title: Water crisis looms in Middle East after strikes on desalination plants | The World | ABC News
description: There could be another dark chapter to the war in the Middle East and that's to do with humankind's most basic need -  water. Iran, which has been suffering severe water shortages has accused the United States of striking one of its desalination plants. Tehran is now threatening to strike the Gulf's water infrastructure if the US hits its energy facilities. So is water another frontier in this war?   Kaveh Madani, director of the United Nations University Institute for Water, Environment and Health and the former deputy head of Iran's environment department spoke to The World's Girish Sawlani about the prospect of desalination plants becoming new targets in this conflict. title: Israeli forces to occupy southern Lebanon up to Litani River | ABC NEWS
description: Israel has confirmed its intention to occupy Lebanese territory south of the Litani River, as Defence Minister Israel Katz says the move is designed to guarantee security for residents of northern Israel.  Israeli forces have blown up many bridges over the river to cut off Hezbollah’s supply lines.  More than 1 million Lebanese residents have been displaced in their country since Israel and Hezbollah began trading fire over attacks on Iran. title: Trump flags 'big present' from Iran as more US troops deployed to Middle East | ABC NEWS
description: US President Donald Trump claims Iran has sent a 'very big present' during a peace process with the White House, while Tehran continues to deny indications of talks with Washington.  The US leader made the comment despite reports 3,000 paratroopers are being deployed to join thousands of marines and navy personnel in the Middle East. title: Why Iran thinks it’s winning the war | ABC News Daily podcast
description: Donald Trump says Iran is negotiating with the US to end the war, but Iran says that’s ‘fake news’.   So, are there efforts underway to bring the conflict to a close or not? And who’s winning the war?   Today, Jonathan Panikoff from the Atlantic Council’s Middle East program on Iran’s stronghold on the Strait of Hormuz and why it will only stop the fighting on its terms.    Featured:    Jonathan Panikoff, director of the Scowcroft Middle East Security Initiative at the Atlantic Council

title: Israel says it is planning to occupy southern Lebanon | ABC NEWS
description: Israel's defence minister Israel Katz has announced plans to take control of southern Lebanon.  He added that the military had destroyed five bridges over the Litani River because he claimed Hezbollah was using them.  Hundreds of thousands of people internally displaced will not be allowed to return to their homes, many fearing an ongoing occupation mirroring the last one from 1982 to 2000. title: Ukraine hit with record drone barrage, Zelenskyy accuses Putin of aiding Iran | ABC NEWS
description: At least five people have been killed, a world hertiage site damaged and dozens injured in Russia’s largest barrage of drone strikes on Ukraine since the Russian invasion began.  US-brokered peace talks between Moscow and Kyiv have been stagnant since the Middle East war broke out.  Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has accused Russian counterpart Vladimir Putin of aiding Iran to strike US-linked targets across the Persian Gulf. title: Mass disruptions on ABC network after thousands of staff go on 24-hour strike | ABC NEWS
description: Thousands of ABC staff have walked off the job to demand better pay and working conditions in the national broadcaster’s first strike in 20 years.  60 per cent of ABC employees who voted on the Enterprise Bargaining Agreement rejected a pay rise of 10 per cent over three years.  ABC Managing Director Hugh Marks has apologised to audiences and staff while Media, Entertainment and Arts Alliance (MEAA)’s Erin Madeley says the union wants to reach a deal that ensures quality and secure work for journalists. title: TC Narelle could reach category 2 or 3 ahead of re-entry in WA | ABC NEWS
description: Tropical Cyclone Narelle has re-intensified to a category 1 system after battering northern Australia from east to west.  The system is expected to track along the Pilbara coast and strengthen to a category 2 or 3 status.  Residents of Western Australia’s west coast are bracing for damaging winds and heavy rain ahead of the storm’s expected third coastal crossing.
